title: Design Twitter
problem_statement: Design a simplified social media service that supports posting tweets, following/unfollowing users, and retrieving the 10 most recent tweets in a user's news feed.

examples.explanation: User 1 follows user 2 and sees the two most recent tweets from themselves and user 2.

constraints: Each user's feed returns at most 10 tweets
intuition: Track each user's tweets with timestamps and maintain each user's followee set so news feed retrieval can merge the latest tweets efficiently.

approaches.name: Timestamped Tweets with Followee Set
approaches.description: Store user tweets with a global timestamp, maintain follow relationships, and use a priority queue to collect the most recent tweets from followed users.
approaches.time: O(F * log T + K log F) for feed retrieval
approaches.why_not: Storing a global sorted feed is expensive and harder to maintain when users follow or unfollow others dynamically.

interview_qa.q: How do you build a news feed efficiently?
interview_qa.a: Store tweets per user with timestamps and merge the latest tweets from followed users when building the feed.

interview_qa.q: What data structures support the follow/unfollow operations?
interview_qa.a: Use a set of followees for each user so add, remove, and membership checks stay efficient.
